summary: Iter99 — Phase 2 Transport Lifecycle + GPS + H3 Heatmap: 24/24 PASS on new test_iter99_transport_lifecycle.py. Iter97 KYC regression: 28/28 PASS. Total 52/52 green. All GPS validations, state-machine transitions, duplicate-active-ride guard (409), access control (403), wallet flow on /complete, ride detail payload, admin heatmap (pickup+dropoff), and schema audit (16 columns + dropped FK) verified end-to-end on the public preview URL.

minor.issue: First cold call took ~45s because ensure_ride_geo_ddl runs multiple ALTER + backfill statements on a freshly-restarted backend. Subsequent calls are <200ms. Not blocking but worth caching the _ddl_done flag across requests (already global in services/transport_geo.py — OK).

critical_code_review_comments: routes/transport.py L297-L300 — _maybe_position_args() does NOT call is_valid_coord when only one of lat/lng is provided. It short-circuits to (None,None,None). Fine (optional ping), but a payload with lat=1.0 and lng=None silently drops both; consider logging a debug warning. Non-blocking.

routes/transport.py L332-L358 — /start accepts both 'en_route' AND 'accepted' as valid predecessors. This is intentional (skip-en-route UX) but means started_at can come before en_route_at is NULL. Confirmed working, but the detail endpoint should surface this gracefully (it does via optional .isoformat()).
routes/transport.py L443-L476 — cancel is idempotent per role (rider/driver). Good. But the notification payload doesn't include ride_id as data — consider adding it so the client app can deep-link.

services/transport_geo.py L104 `_ddl_done` is a module-global flag. Works because uvicorn runs one process, but will miss a re-bootstrap if the Neon schema is manually truncated. Low-risk accepted cost.
routes/transport.py L1012-L1015 — the heatmap try/except silently `continue`s on invalid cells. For prod observability add a warning log so malformed H3 cells (from legacy backfill) don't hide.
routes/transport.py L886 lazy-imports h3 inside the loop. Prefer hoisting `from services.transport_geo import h3` to module top to avoid the repeated import overhead on large result sets.

Duplicate haversine implementation: services/transport_geo.haversine_km is imported but NEVER used in routes/transport.py — the local haversine_km at L74 is used instead. Consider removing one (DRY) — prefer the shared helper.

Potential race: POST /request checks `active` then INSERTs without a row lock — two concurrent requests from the same rider could both pass the check. Add UNIQUE partial index on ride_requests(rider_id) WHERE status IN ('pending','accepted','en_route','started') for bullet-proof idempotency.
test_credentials: admin@japap.com, alice@japap.com (driver), bob@japap.com (rider). JWT minted with JWT_SECRET from backend/.env. Bob wallet set to 50000 XAF in _setup().
seed_data_creation: Test creates Alice driver + approves her, Bob wallet=50000 XAF, creates ~3 rides during the lifecycle run. Cleanup fixture deletes ride_requests, notifications (type LIKE 'ride_%'), driver_kyc_decisions, drivers for alice+bob at module teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 2 Transport (iter99) fully validated. All 15 requirements from review_request (GPS validation, lifecycle transitions, transition guards 409, wallet split on complete, ride detail payload, heatmap pickup/dropoff, schema self-heal 16 cols, FK dropped) are green. Iter97 KYC regression also re-run green (28/28). For next iter, focus on the frontend lifecycle UI (driver 'En route' / 'Start' / 'Complete' buttons, rider tracking view with driver_position polling every 5s) — backend is ready.
rca of the issue: No bug to RCA — all happy paths and negative cases pass on first run. One transient issue: first backend call after supervisor reload takes ~45s due to the multi-statement schema self-heal; mitigated in the test fixture with a /api/health warm-up + 90s timeout + 3-retry. The backend code is correct; this is just a cold-start observation.
